completed_tasks.name: Multi-user data isolation — users/sessions tables, session_id cookie, all queries scoped by user_id
Fix DB migration — drop legacy account_id tables, recreate with user_id INTEGER FK
Fix auth redirect — new users go to / not /no-project (route not in React Router)

remaining_tasks.name: Verify login works end-to-end — clear cookies → OAuth → land on ConnectionPage with no 500 error
Verify sync — add Jira project → sync completes without 'Sync failed'
UAT: two browser profiles, different Atlassian accounts → confirm isolated project lists
UAT: Bug Report Word (.docx) and Sprint Word (.docx) exports
Responsive UI polish UAT: sidebar animations, card layouts, navbar logo

blockers.description: Login still unconfirmed — user paused before testing fixes
blockers.workaround: Clear browser cookies for localhost → go to http://localhost:3000 → Login → Atlassian OAuth → should land on ConnectionPage (no 500). If 500: powershell.exe -Command "docker logs group2_warnnoi300percent-backend-1 --tail 30"

human_actions_pending.action: Clear browser cookies for localhost (remove old jira_session or session_id cookie), log in fresh via Atlassian OAuth, add a Jira project, confirm sync completes
human_actions_pending.context: DB migration ran on last restart. New auth uses session_id cookie (opaque token → user_id lookup in sessions table). Old jira_session cookie is stale.

decisions.decision: Proper users+sessions tables with INTEGER user_id FK on all data tables
decisions.rationale: Codebase was already refactored to this model. database.py was the only laggard — still created tables with legacy account_id TEXT. Fixed by migration that drops+recreates legacy tables on startup.

decisions.decision: session_id cookie (opaque token) not jira_session (account_id value)
decisions.rationale: Proper session abstraction. session_id → user_id lookup in sessions table. Safer than storing account_id directly in cookie.
/no-project not in React Router — triggers *-catch-all. Direct to / (ConnectionPage) instead.
backend/ and frontend/src/ show equal insertion/deletion counts — pure CRLF noise from WSL/Windows. No semantic changes.

next_action: User clears browser cookies → logs in via Atlassian OAuth → verifies no 500 error, lands on ConnectionPage → adds Jira project → verifies sync completes. If error: powershell.exe -Command "docker logs group2_warnnoi300percent-backend-1 --tail 30"
context_notes: Root cause of 500 on login: codebase was refactored to users+sessions+integer user_id FK BEFORE database.py was updated. So init_db() still created tables with legacy account_id TEXT. On OAuth callback, upsert_oauth_token(user_id, ...) failed with 'table oauth_tokens has no column named user_id'. Fixed with migration that checks for account_id in table SQL and drops+recreates. All 4 data tables now correct (user_id=True, account_id=False). Backend running clean on last check. Auth chain: OAuth → /me → upsert_user(atlassian_account_id) → user_id int → create_session(user_id) → session_id cookie → all subsequent requests use Depends(get_current_user) which reads session_id cookie.
